title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
summary: Jack Roberts presents the /goal command in OpenAI's Codex, which enables autonomous code generation by taking a high-level objective and iterating through planning, execution, and self-correction without user intervention. The Ralph Loop automatically handles feature builds, bug fixes, and scaffolding from a single sentence prompt.
writeup: Jack Roberts walks through the /goal command in OpenAI's Codex, which automates code generation through a self-correcting loop. The workflow starts with a plain-English description of the desired outcome, after which the Ralph Loop plans, executes, tests, and self-corrects without manual intervention. The video matters because it shows a shift from prompting individual edits toward delegating an entire build-and-fix cycle to the model. The concrete takeaway: a single goal description can drive an automated plan, execute, test, and correct sequence end to end.
